title: lepidotheca aurea
intro: Lepidotheca aurea, commonly known as the "Golden Chain Fern", is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Lepidotheca aurea has been reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as promoting wound healing and reducing oxidative stress.
side_effects: Lepidotheca aurea can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues due to its volatile oils. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while excessive consumption may result in headaches and dizziness. Improper use of Lepidotheca aurea may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse effects. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction.
